mappings.rationaleAI: Organized profit-seeking activity covering production organization (A), accounting (H.1), financial management (H.2), human resources (H.3), production administration (H.4), and distribution of goods (H.5).
Financial intermediary covering historical development of banking (D.2.a), modern banking structure (D.2.b), commercial banking principles (D.2.c), and the money market (D.2.e).
Medium of exchange covering basic functions (D.1.a), various forms from commodity to fiat (D.1.b), and quantity theory of money across classical and Keynesian frameworks (D.1.c).
Individual firm decision-making covering the production function, substitution, and marginal cost analysis in the short run (A.1) and long run profit maximization (A.2).
Discipline of monetary resource management covering the market for long-term funds including securities and pension funds (D.2.g) and management of business funds (H.2).
Price determination model relevant to the relationship of marginal cost to market price (A.1) and earnings of land, labor, and capital (B.2).
Government-funded support systems constituting the rise in social welfare expenditures (G.3).
Legal entity with board governance, directly constituting the business corporation (D.4) and addressing consolidations and mergers (H.2.c).
Extraction of geological materials constituting land and raw materials as inputs in the productive process (C.2).
Worker organization for collective bargaining, directly constituting the organization of unions (C.1.g) and the influence of unions on labor supply, wages, and output (C.1.h).
Final-stage sale of goods to consumers, directly constituting retailing as a method of distributing goods (H.5.b).
Risk-management mechanism covering the nature of insurance (J.1), fire and marine (J.2), casualty and surety (J.3), life and health (J.4), underwriting and rate making (J.6), and legal aspects (J.7).
Transfer of goods through market networks covering theories of international trade (F.1) and national factors including tariffs and production conditions (F.2).
Division of labor and free markets relevant to production organization (A), relation between production and incomes (B.1), and classical theories of international trade (F.1).
Agricultural development from Neolithic through Green Revolution covering the relationship between agriculture and economic development (E.1) and the effect of technology on world agriculture (E.4).
Private ownership system with profit motive organizing production (A), with the corporate form as a key institutional arrangement (D.4).
Customer acquisition and retention strategies constituting marketing department functions (H.5.a), market research (H.5.e), and advertising (I).
Mandatory government levy directly constituting the role of taxation in the budgetary process (G.1).
Cultivation of crops and livestock covering farm price behavior (E.3), technology's effect on agriculture (E.4), and types of farms (E.5).
Movement of goods across space relevant to flow channels of information and materials in production (H.4.a).
Movement of people relevant to the economic and social status of temporary and seasonal migrant laborers (C.1.f).
Agricultural input driving crop yield increases central to the Green Revolution, relevant to the effect of technology on world agriculture (E.4).
Physical systems enabling societal functioning, constituting institutional arrangements that facilitate production and output (D).
Critique of surplus value addressing the relation between productive process and incomes (B.1), and labor theory of value relevant to labor as an input (C.1).
Intensifying economic interdependence driving international trade arrangements (F.3) and the geographical distribution of resources and markets (F).
Productive activity as a primary factor of production covering labor force size and deployment (C.1.a) and employment and unemployment (C.1.e).
Administration of organizations covering the economic role of managers and entrepreneurs (C.1.j), management of human resources (H.3), and administration of production (H.4).
